project.title: Quantitative Profiling of Protein S-Glutathionylation during Nanoparticle-Induced Oxidative Stress
project.projectDescription: Quantitative profiling of protein s-glutathionylation (SSG) reveals redox-dependent regulation of macrophage function during nanoparticle-induced oxidative stress
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Treated cells with 3 different nanoparticles (SiO2, Fe3O4, CoO) then enriched for SSG nanoparticles.  Samples were labeled with 6-plex TMT then analyzed by LC-MS/MS
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Data was searched with MSGF+ using PNNL's DMS Processing pipeline

references.referenceLine: Duan J, Kodali VK, Gaffrey MJ, Guo J, Chu RK, Camp DG, Smith RD, Thrall BD, Qian WJ. Quantitative Profiling of Protein S-Glutathionylation Reveals Redox-Dependent Regulation of Macrophage Function during Nanoparticle-Induced Oxidative Stress. ACS Nano. 2016 Jan 26;10(1):524-38

Comment[Instrument]: Thermo Scientific LTQ-Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific, San Jose, CA)
Comment[CollisionEnergy]: with a normalized collision energy setting of 45.
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: 20 ppm tolerance for precursor ion masses
